response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with a strong craftsmanship background, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, German), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switch development. C experience is limited to a brief 7-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product development, not embedded firmware.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded-adjacent work
Prior C/C++ development on developer tooling (NI LabWindows/CVI) and low-latency systems work in DLang
Strong Linux, testing/TDD, and CI/CD practices with a track record of knowledge sharing
concerns: No indication of Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native level)
No Linux kernel source-modification, embedded networking, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, far from embedded LAN-switch firmware in C

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Solid general engineering and some C/Linux background
